reasoning: The Lean signature universally quantifies over f, g : Polynomial ℚ (via implicit arguments) and over coefficient indices i, j : ℕ (via theorem parameters), matching the informal "for any polynomials with rational coefficients" and "any coefficient of f with any coefficient of g". The hypothesis `∀ n : ℕ, ∃ a : ℤ, (f * g).coeff n = a` correctly encodes "f*g has integer coefficients" using the standard Mathlib idiom of existence of an integer equal (via coercion to ℚ) to the rational coefficient. The conclusion `∃ a : ℤ, f.coeff i * g.coeff j = a` similarly encodes that the product of arbitrary coefficients is an integer. Types and quantifier structure align precisely with the informal statement.
